title: Oreogrammitis interrupta
intro: Oreogrammitis interrupta, also known as the interrupted lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Oreogrammitis interrupta contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis interrupta is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oreogrammitis and the phylum Pteridophyta. Within the kingdom Plantae, this species is classified as a vascular plant. The division Pteridophyta is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores, and Oreogrammitis interrupta is a part of this division. This classification places Oreogrammitis interrupta within the plant kingdom and its unique characteristics within the fern family.
